2	  "body": "**Uberon term**\n[life cycle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000104)\n[life cycle stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000105)\n\n**Suggested revision and reasons**\nCOB had a discussion about various occurrent subclasses such as UBERON's 'life cycle', and the suggestion for COB compatibility was to move 'life cycle' and 'life cycle stage' from its current \"processual entity\" parent to be under \"process\" instead, thus helping eliminate \"occurent\" class in COB integrated ontologies.\n\nI see however COB still needs to find a home for [life cycle temporal boundary](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0035943) .  I think COB needs to recognize use of some OWLTime classes directly, where this would find a home, or indirectly by importing BFO \"temporal region\" as well. \n\n",
3	  "comments": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd86a7BG4",
6	      "author": {
7	        "login": "cmungall"
8	      },
9	      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
10	      "body": "Do you want to make a COB ticket or place alongside this?\n \n- https://github.com/OBOFoundry/COB/issues/40\n\nI don't think Uberon cares about upper level representation of this, we have 3 terms that appear to be useless vestiges of the heady days of over-formalization:\n\n* [] UBERON:0000000 ! processual entity \"An occurrent [span:Occurrent] that exists in time by occurring or happening, has temporal parts and always involves and depends on some entity.\"\n    * [i] **UBERON:0035943 ! life cycle temporal boundary \"A temporal boundary connecting two life cycle stages that follow in immediate succession. A temporal boundary is an abstract, instantaneous entity.\"**\n        * [i] UBERON:0035946 ! start of neonate stage \"A life cycle temporal boundary that marks the start of the neonate stage of the organism.\"\n        * [i] UBERON:0035945 ! start of life cycle \"A life cycle temporal boundary that marks the start of the life cycle of the organism.\"\n        * [i] UBERON:0035944 ! life-death temporal boundary \"A life cycle temporal boundary that marks the end of the life cycle of the organism.\"\n\nthese strange sounding terms might be better colloquially called conception, birth, death. IMO 0D is overmodeling, we are better just treating these concepts analogous to how we treat 3D boundaries in uberon, as actually having some extent, albeit minimal",

IMO 0D is overmodeling, we are better just treating these concepts analogous to how we treat 3D boundaries in uberon, as actually having some extent, albeit minimal\n\nMight be worth noting also that unless I am missing something, neither '[life cycle temporal boundary](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0035943)' nor any of its 3 children are actually _used_ anywhere within Uberon. They are also _not_ used by any of [the taxon-specific life-stage ontologies](https://github.com/obophenotype/developmental-stage-ontologies).",

content.content:
format-version: 1.2
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"
synonymtypedef: BRAIN_NAME_ABV "brain name abbreviations (drosophila)"
synonymtypedef: COMPARATIVE_PREFERRED "preferred term in the context of comparative anatomy"
synonymtypedef: DEPRECATED "A historic synonym, no longer encouraged"
synonymtypedef: DEVELOPMENTAL "developmental term"
synonymtypedef: HOMOLOGY "a synonym made on the basis of a possibly homologous structure in another species"
synonymtypedef: HUMAN_PREFERRED "preferred term when talking about an instance of this class in Homo sapiens"
synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"
synonymtypedef: NON_AMNIOTE "synonym that is inappropriate for amniotes"
synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003000 "abbreviation"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003002 "dubious synonym"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003004 "plural form"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003011 "latin term"
synonymtypedef: PENDING_REVIEW "expert consultation and attribution required"
synonymtypedef: SENSU "taxonomic disambiguation"
synonymtypedef: SYSTEMATIC "a systematic synonym used as the base name for design patterns"

input.content:
[Term]
id: UBERON:0035943
name: obsolete life cycle temporal boundary
def: "A temporal boundary connecting two life cycle stages that follow in immediate succession. A temporal boundary is an abstract, instantaneous entity." [https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165]
comment: This term and its subclasses (life-death temporal boundary, start of life cycle, start of neonate stage) were obsoleted as vestiges of over-formalization. They were never used within Uberon nor by taxon-specific developmental-stage ontologies. Concepts like conception, birth and death are better represented by the corresponding life cycle stages (e.g. zygote stage, neonate stage, death stage), treated analogously to 3D boundaries in Uberon as having some minimal extent rather than as abstract 0D instantaneous entities. See https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3464
is_obsolete: true
consider: UBERON:0000104

git add src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Obsolete vestigial life cycle temporal boundary terms

Obsoletes the following 4 terms which were identified in
obophenotype/uberon#3464 as vestiges of over-formalization,
confirmed by curators as never used within Uberon or by
taxon-specific developmental-stage ontologies:

- UBERON:0035943 'life cycle temporal boundary' -> consider UBERON:0000104 (life cycle)
- UBERON:0035944 'life-death temporal boundary' -> consider UBERON:0000071 (death stage)
- UBERON:0035945 'start of life cycle' -> consider UBERON:0000106 (zygote stage)
- UBERON:0035946 'start of neonate stage' -> consider UBERON:0007221 (neonate stage)

The colloquial concepts of conception, birth and death are
better represented by the corresponding life cycle stages,
treated analogously to 3D boundaries in Uberon as having some
minimal extent rather than as abstract 0D instantaneous entities.

All logical axioms (is_a, intersection_of, relationship) have
been removed from the obsoleted terms; consider tags point to
suitable conceptual replacements.

Signed-off-by: dragon-ai-agent

Refs: https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3464
EOF

# PR: Obsolete vestigial life cycle temporal boundary terms

Partial resolution for issue #3464.

## Summary

This PR obsoletes the four "life cycle temporal boundary" terms that the issue
discussion identified as useless vestiges of over-formalization. It does **not**
attempt the broader re-parenting of `life cycle` and `life cycle stage` under
`process` (see *Scope* below).

| Term | Status | `consider` |
|------|--------|-----------|
| UBERON:0035943 `life cycle temporal boundary` | obsoleted | UBERON:0000104 (`life cycle`) |
| UBERON:0035944 `life-death temporal boundary` | obsoleted | UBERON:0000071 (`death stage`) |
| UBERON:0035945 `start of life cycle` | obsoleted | UBERON:0000106 (`zygote stage`) |
| UBERON:0035946 `start of neonate stage` | obsoleted | UBERON:0007221 (`neonate stage`) |

## Rationale

From the issue discussion:

- @cmungall (issue author): "we have 3 terms that appear to be useless vestiges
  of the heady days of over-formalization ... these strange sounding terms might
  be better colloquially called conception, birth, death. IMO 0D is overmodeling,
  we are better just treating these concepts analogous to how we treat 3D
  boundaries in uberon, as actually having some extent, albeit minimal".
- @gouttegd: confirmed that "neither 'life cycle temporal boundary' nor any of
  its 3 children are actually used anywhere within Uberon. They are also not
  used by any of the taxon-specific life-stage ontologies".

A `grep` over `uberon-edit.obo` confirms the only references to these four IDs
are within their own stanzas (parent/child relationships among the four), so no
rewiring of other terms is required.

The `consider` tags point to the corresponding life cycle stages — these are the
concepts curators are most likely to want when they encounter the obsolete IDs.

## Scope: what this PR does NOT do

The original title of #3464 is *Positioning 'life cycle' and 'life cycle stage'
under 'process'*. This part is **deliberately left alone** because:

1. There is no `process` (BFO:0000015) class currently in Uberon —
   `processual entity` (UBERON:0000000, mapped to `BFO:0000003` /
   `occurrent`) is the only upper-level class available. Pulling in
   BFO:0000015 needs separate consideration.
2. @cmungall noted "I don't think Uberon cares about upper level representation
   of this" and the most recent comment on the ticket was "We should hopefully
   reach resolution soon on the linked ticket [COB#40] then I think we can close
   this?" — i.e. the re-parenting decision is parked on COB.
3. UBERON:0035943 was the only `life cycle`-adjacent term that was both an
   unambiguous vestige and free of downstream consumers; obsoleting it is
   orthogonal to the COB decision.

## Checklist

- [x] Read issue #3464 in full (body + 4 comments)
- [x] Inspected each of the 4 target terms in `uberon-edit.obo`
- [x] Confirmed no in-edit-file references to the 4 IDs outside their own stanzas
- [x] Used `obo-checkout.pl` / `obo-checkin.pl` workflow per CLAUDE.md
- [x] All logical axioms (`is_a`, `intersection_of`, `relationship`) removed
      from each obsoleted term (per CLAUDE.md obsoletion guidance)
- [x] Each obsolete term has `is_obsolete: true`, an explanatory `comment:`, and
      a `consider:` tag pointing to a sensible conceptual replacement
- [x] Names prefixed with `obsolete ` per the example in CLAUDE.md
- [x] Synonyms retained where still useful for text lookup ("birth", "death",
      etc.)
- [x] Reserialized `src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo` with
      `robot convert -i ... -f obo -o ...`
- [x] Committed only the intended file (`src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo`)

## Notes for reviewers

- The broader re-parenting question stays open in #3464; this PR is a partial
  cleanup, not a closer.
- If you would prefer the obsoleted terms point to multiple `consider` tags
  (e.g. `UBERON:0035943` -> both `life cycle` and `life cycle stage`), or
  prefer no `consider` at all on the abstract grouping class, that is a
  one-line tweak.
